findings.title: Template-imported AI to-do places (source:'ai') are silently DELETED on the new owner's first Accept Plan
findings.rootCause: This is a direct sibling of the just-fixed Accept-Plan/markedPlaces data-loss bug. A creator who builds an itinerary with the AI planner gets markedPlaces stamped `source:'ai'` AND `forManual:true` (markedPlaces.ts:314-320 in addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified) — so they show in the to-do list. _build_template_snapshot/_clean_marked_places (templates.py:85-109,182-186) copy every field EXCEPT dayId/timeOfDay, so `source:'ai'` is preserved verbatim into the snapshot. _instantiate_template (templates.py:220,251) re-runs _clean_marked_places and writes the places straight into the new trip's marked_places_json with `source:'ai'` intact. The new owner has no idea these are 'ai'-tagged. On their first Accept Plan, useAiPlan.ts:459 calls dropAITaggedPlaces(activeTrip) which filters out EVERY `source==='ai'` place (markedPlaces.ts:345-348) — deleting the imported template content. The recent fix only stopped addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified from PROMOTING a manual place to 'ai'; it did nothing for places that arrive already stamped 'ai' from a template (or clone — _clone_trip_record copies marked_places_json verbatim too, trips.py:1307-1309).

findings.title: Instantiated template (and clone) day rows keep NULL dates that never populate when the new owner sets a trip date range
findings.rootCause: _instantiate_template inserts every day with date=NULL (templates.py:285,290) — by design, matching _clone_trip_record which NULLs dates and whose comment (trips.py:1395-1406) explicitly states the frontend is supposed to populate fresh dates when the new owner picks a start. But the Edit-Trip date logic can't do that: it pre-fills the picker from numberedDays[0].date (trip.ts:319) which is '' for an instantiated trip, and its rebase/extend branches are gated on `startChanged = newStart && oldFirst && ...` / `endChanged = newEnd && oldLast && ...` (trip.ts:532-535). oldFirst/oldLast come from parseUTC(numberedDays[0|N].date || '') which is null when the days have no dates, so both guards short-circuit to false and NO date is ever written to the day rows (the only path that scaffolds fresh dates is the numberedDays.length===0 branch, which doesn't apply here because the days exist). The documented clone/template date-assignment contract is therefore broken for both clone and template instantiation.

findings.title: Public template preview counts the internal Trip-Hub anchor as a day (off-by-one 'days planned') and renders it as a ★ row to strangers
findings.rootCause: _build_template_snapshot selects ALL non-deleted trip_days ORDER BY day_number with no `day_number > 0` filter (templates.py:162-167), so the snapshot's `days` array includes the lazily-created Trip-Hub anchor (dayNumber 0; created client-side in pages/home-mount/handlers.ts:246-260, so it exists for any trip the creator has opened on Home). fetch_template_preview then sets dayCount = len(days) (templates.py:527), counting day 0, and passes day 0 through to template.html, which renders dayNumber==0 as a ★ 'Trip Hub' row (template.html:106-111). The anchor usually has empty plan slots so it shows as a contentless extra row.
